sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.emp_fname ,  T3.prof_high_degree FROM CLASS AS T1 JOIN employee AS T2 ON T1.prof_num  =  T2.emp_num JOIN professor AS T3 ON T2.emp_num  =  T3.emp_num JOIN department AS T4 ON T4.dept_code  =  T3.dept_code WHERE T4.dept_name  =  'Computer Info. Systems'

text: How many faculty lines are there in the university that conferred the least number of degrees in year 2001?

text: What are the names of customers who have purchased both products Latte and Americano?

sql: SELECT t1.customer_name FROM customers AS t1 JOIN customer_orders AS t2 ON t1.customer_id  =  t2.customer_id JOIN order_items AS t3 ON t2.order_id  =  t3.order_id JOIN products AS t4 ON t3.product_id  =  t4.product_id WHERE t4.product_details  =  'Latte' INTERSECT SELECT t1.customer_name FROM customers AS t1 JOIN customer_orders AS t2 ON t1.customer_id  =  t2.customer_id JOIN order_items AS t3 ON t2.order_id  =  t3.order_id JOIN products AS t4 ON t3.product_id  =  t4.product_id WHERE t4.product_details  =  'Americano'

sql: SELECT count(DISTINCT city) FROM Person

text: Show the leader names and locations of colleges.
structured: SELECT Leader_Name ,  College_Location FROM college
sql: SELECT Leader_Name ,  College_Location FROM college

text: Find the name of the customer who made the order of the largest amount of goods.
structured: SELECT t1.customer_name FROM customers AS t1 JOIN customer_orders AS t2 ON t1.customer_id  =  t2.customer_id JOIN order_items AS t3 ON t2.order_id  =  t3.order_id WHERE t3.order_quantity  =  ( SELECT max(order_quantity) FROM order_items)
sql: SELECT t1.customer_name FROM customers AS t1 JOIN customer_orders AS t2 ON t1.customer_id  =  t2.customer_id JOIN order_items AS t3 ON t2.order_id  =  t3.order_id WHERE t3.order_quantity  =  ( SELECT max(order_quantity) FROM order_items)

text: How many distinct locations of perpetrators are there?
structured: SELECT count(DISTINCT LOCATION) FROM perpetrator
sql: SELECT count(DISTINCT LOCATION) FROM perpetrator

text: What are the first names of the teachers who teach grade 1?
structured: SELECT DISTINCT T2.firstname FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.classroom  =  T2.classroom WHERE grade  =  1
sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.firstname FROM list AS T1 JOIN teachers AS T2 ON T1.classroom  =  T2.classroom WHERE grade  =  1

text: Find the names of the customers who have an deputy policy.
structured: SELECT DISTINCT t2.customer_details FROM policies AS t1 JOIN customers AS t2 ON t1.customer_id  =  t2.customer_id WHERE t1.policy_type_code  =  "Deputy"
sql: SELECT DISTINCT t2.customer_details FROM policies AS t1 JOIN customers AS t2 ON t1.customer_id  =  t2.customer_id WHERE t1.policy_type_code  =  "Deputy"

text: How many stadiums are not in country "Russia"?
structured: SELECT count(*) FROM stadium WHERE country != 'Russia'
sql: SELECT count(*) FROM stadium WHERE country != 'Russia'

text: How many students are older than 20 in each dorm?
structured: SELECT count(*) ,  T3.dorm_name FROM student AS T1 JOIN lives_in AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid JOIN dorm AS T3 ON T3.dormid  =  T2.dormid WHERE T1.age  >  20 GROUP BY T3.dorm_name
sql: SELECT count(*) ,  T3.dorm_name FROM student AS T1 JOIN lives_in AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid JOIN dorm AS T3 ON T3.dormid  =  T2.dormid WHERE T1.age  >  20 GROUP BY T3.dorm_name

text: Give me the theme and location of each party.
structured: SELECT Party_Theme ,  LOCATION FROM party
sql: SELECT Party_Theme ,  LOCATION FROM party

text: Which department has the lowest budget?
structured: SELECT dept_name FROM department ORDER BY budget LIMIT 1
sql: SELECT dept_name FROM department ORDER BY budget LIMIT 1

text: Show the investors shared by entrepreneurs that requested more than 140000 and entrepreneurs that requested less than 120000.
structured: SELECT Investor FROM entrepreneur WHERE Money_Requested  >  140000 INTERSECT SELECT Investor FROM entrepreneur WHERE Money_Requested  <  120000
sql: SELECT Investor FROM entrepreneur WHERE Money_Requested  >  140000 INTERSECT SELECT Investor FROM entrepreneur WHERE Money_Requested  <  120000

text: Find all invoice dates corresponding to customers with first name Astrid and last name Gruber.
structured: SELECT T2.InvoiceDate FROM CUSTOMER AS T1 JOIN INVOICE AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId  =  T2.CustomerId WHERE T1.FirstName  =  "Astrid" AND LastName  =  "Gruber"
sql: SELECT T2.InvoiceDate FROM CUSTOMER AS T1 JOIN INVOICE AS T2 ON T1.CustomerId  =  T2.CustomerId WHERE T1.FirstName  =  "Astrid" AND LastName  =  "Gruber"

text: Show names for all aircrafts of which John Williams has certificates.
structured: SELECT T3.name FROM Employee AS T1 JOIN Certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid  =  T2.eid JOIN Aircraft AS T3 ON T3.aid  =  T2.aid WHERE T1.name  =  "John Williams"
sql: SELECT T3.name FROM Employee AS T1 JOIN Certificate AS T2 ON T1.eid  =  T2.eid JOIN Aircraft AS T3 ON T3.aid  =  T2.aid WHERE T1.name  =  "John Williams"

text: For each  airport name, how many routes start at that airport, ordered from most to least?
structured: SELECT count(*) ,  T1.name FROM airports AS T1 JOIN routes AS T2 ON T1.apid  =  T2.src_apid GROUP BY T1.name ORDER BY count(*) DESC
sql: SELECT count(*) ,  T1.name FROM airports AS T1 JOIN routes AS T2 ON T1.apid  =  T2.src_apid GROUP BY T1.name ORDER BY count(*) DESC

text: How many students are there for each major?
structured: SELECT major ,  count(*) FROM Student GROUP BY major
sql: SELECT major ,  count(*) FROM Student GROUP BY major

text: Find the names of the companies whose headquarters are not located in "USA".
structured: SELECT name FROM Companies WHERE Headquarters != 'USA'
sql: SELECT name FROM Companies WHERE Headquarters != 'USA'

text: List the phone numbers of all employees.
structured: SELECT Phone FROM EMPLOYEE
sql: SELECT Phone FROM EMPLOYEE

text: What are the statuses and average populations of each city?
structured: SELECT Status ,  avg(Population) FROM city GROUP BY Status
sql: SELECT Status ,  avg(Population) FROM city GROUP BY Status

text: What are the numbers of all flights coming from Los Angeles?
structured: SELECT flno FROM Flight WHERE origin  =  "Los Angeles"
sql: SELECT flno FROM Flight WHERE origin  =  "Los Angeles"

text: On which day has it neither been foggy nor rained in the zip code of 94107?
structured: SELECT date FROM weather WHERE zip_code  =  94107 AND EVENTS != "Fog" AND EVENTS != "Rain"
sql: SELECT date FROM weather WHERE zip_code  =  94107 AND EVENTS != "Fog" AND EVENTS != "Rain"

text: Which paper has the most authors? Give me the paper title.
structured: SELECT t2.title FROM authorship AS t1 JOIN papers AS t2 ON t1.paperid  =  t2.paperid WHERE t1.authorder  =  (SELECT max(authorder) FROM authorship)
sql: SELECT t2.title FROM authorship AS t1 JOIN papers AS t2 ON t1.paperid  =  t2.paperid WHERE t1.authorder  =  (SELECT max(authorder) FROM authorship)

text: Return the date of birth for all the guests with gender code "Male".
structured: SELECT date_of_birth FROM Guests WHERE gender_code  =  "Male"
sql: SELECT date_of_birth FROM Guests WHERE gender_code  =  "Male"

text: Find the average credit score of the customers who have some loan.
structured: SELECT avg(credit_score) FROM customer WHERE cust_id IN (SELECT cust_id FROM loan)
sql: SELECT avg(credit_score) FROM customer WHERE cust_id IN (SELECT cust_id FROM loan)

text: Which origin has most number of flights?
structured: SELECT origin FROM Flight GROUP BY origin ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
sql: SELECT origin FROM Flight GROUP BY origin ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1

text: What is the maximum Online Mendelian Inheritance in Man (OMIM) value of the enzymes?
structured: SELECT max(OMIM) FROM enzyme
sql: SELECT max(OMIM) FROM enzyme

text: What are all the characteristic names of product "sesame"?
structured: SELECT t3.characteristic_name FROM products AS t1 JOIN product_characteristics AS t2 ON t1.product_id  =  t2.product_id JOIN CHARACTERISTICS AS t3 ON t2.characteristic_id  =  t3.characteristic_id WHERE t1.product_name  =  "sesame"
sql: SELECT t3.characteristic_name FROM products AS t1 JOIN product_characteristics AS t2 ON t1.product_id  =  t2.product_id JOIN CHARACTERISTICS AS t3 ON t2.characteristic_id  =  t3.characteristic_id WHERE t1.product_name  =  "sesame"

text: Find the id of suppliers whose average amount purchased for each product is above 50000 or below 30000.
structured: SELECT supplier_id FROM Product_Suppliers GROUP BY supplier_id HAVING avg(total_amount_purchased)  >  50000 OR avg(total_amount_purchased)  <  30000
sql: SELECT supplier_id FROM Product_Suppliers GROUP BY supplier_id HAVING avg(total_amount_purchased)  >  50000 OR avg(total_amount_purchased)  <  30000

text: What are the distinct names of nurses on call?

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM nurse AS T1 JOIN on_call AS T2 ON T1.EmployeeID  =  T2.nurse

text: Show the name of employees with three lowest salaries.

text: List all the characteristic names and data types of product "cumin".

sql: SELECT t3.characteristic_name ,  t3.characteristic_data_type FROM products AS t1 JOIN product_characteristics AS t2 ON t1.product_id  =  t2.product_id JOIN CHARACTERISTICS AS t3 ON t2.characteristic_id  =  t3.characteristic_id WHERE t1.product_name  =  "cumin"

text: What is the name of the medication used for the patient staying in room 111?
structured: SELECT T4.name FROM stay AS T1 JOIN patient AS T2 ON T1.Patient  =  T2.SSN JOIN Prescribes AS T3 ON T3.Patient  =  T2.SSN JOIN Medication AS T4 ON T3.Medication  =  T4.Code WHERE room  =  111
sql: SELECT T4.name FROM stay AS T1 JOIN patient AS T2 ON T1.Patient  =  T2.SSN JOIN Prescribes AS T3 ON T3.Patient  =  T2.SSN JOIN Medication AS T4 ON T3.Medication  =  T4.Code WHERE room  =  111

text: Find the names of items whose rank is higher than 3 and whose average rating is above 5.
structured: SELECT T1.title FROM item AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.i_id  =  T2.i_id WHERE T2.rank  >  3 INTERSECT SELECT T1.title FROM item AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.i_id  =  T2.i_id GROUP BY T2.i_id HAVING avg(T2.rating)  >  5
sql: SELECT T1.title FROM item AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.i_id  =  T2.i_id WHERE T2.rank  >  3 INTERSECT SELECT T1.title FROM item AS T1 JOIN review AS T2 ON T1.i_id  =  T2.i_id GROUP BY T2.i_id HAVING avg(T2.rating)  >  5

text: What is the name of the customer who has greatest total loan amount?

sql: SELECT T1.cust_name FROM customer AS T1 JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.cust_id  =  T2.cust_id GROUP BY T1.cust_name ORDER BY sum(T2.amount) DESC LIMIT 1

sql: SELECT T1.Store_Phone ,  T1.Store_Email_Address FROM Drama_Workshop_Groups AS T1 JOIN Services AS T2 ON T1.Workshop_Group_ID  =  T2.Workshop_Group_ID

text: What is the shortest and most poorly rated song for each genre, ordered alphabetically by genre?

text: How many times has the student Linda Smith visited Subway?
structured: SELECT count(*) FROM Student JOIN Visits_Restaurant ON Student.StuID = Visits_Restaurant.StuID JOIN Restaurant ON Visits_Restaurant.ResID = Restaurant.ResID WHERE Student.Fname = "Linda" AND Student.Lname = "Smith" AND Restaurant.ResName = "Subway";
sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Student JOIN Visits_Restaurant ON Student.StuID = Visits_Restaurant.StuID JOIN Restaurant ON Visits_Restaurant.ResID = Restaurant.ResID WHERE Student.Fname = "Linda" AND Student.Lname = "Smith" AND Restaurant.ResName = "Subway";

text: Show the names of editors that are on at least two journal committees.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM editor AS T1 JOIN journal_committee AS T2 ON T1.Editor_ID  =  T2.Editor_ID GROUP BY T1.Name HAVING COUNT(*)  >=  2

text: What is the name, address, and number of students in the departments that have the 3 most students?
structured: SELECT T2.dept_name ,  T2.dept_address ,  count(*) FROM student AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.dept_code  =  T2.dept_code GROUP BY T1.dept_code ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 3
sql: SELECT T2.dept_name ,  T2.dept_address ,  count(*) FROM student AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.dept_code  =  T2.dept_code GROUP BY T1.dept_code ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 3
